CIPHER.520.cipherString: Art and freedom of expression will change the world faster than politics and religion combined.
CIPHER.520.question: Decrypt the following cipher text that has been encoded using the Aristocrat Cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.520.author: Kayambila Mpulamasaka

CIPHER.505.cipherString: Youth is the gift of nature, but age is a work of art.
CIPHER.505.question: This code is found written in chalk on the sidewalk outside the science building. It's a quote by Stanislaw Lec, written in a Baconian Cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.505.author: Stanislaw Lec

CIPHER.519.cipherString: Focus beyond the finish line celebrate incremental progress along the way for sustained motivation!
CIPHER.519.question: Etched into the bottom of the cafeteria's mystery-cookie tray: this Aristocrat quote by Felecia Etienne. What does it say?
CIPHER.519.author: Felecia Etienne

CIPHER.495.cipherString: What is the best way to communicate with a fish? Drop it a line!
CIPHER.495.question: Found inside a dissected frog's stomach (plastic, don’t worry): this Complete Columnar note. The quote has HEBES somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.495.author: Unknown

CIPHER.506.cipherString: Divine love is a sacred flower, which in its early bud is happiness, and in its full bloom is heaven.
CIPHER.506.question: During a field trip, you notice these strange markings on a metal plaque. They represent a quote by Hervey, encoded using an Aristocrat Cipher. You are told that the cipher contains the word IN twice. What does it say?
CIPHER.506.author: Hervey

CIPHER.518.cipherString: I want to be the positive force that heals people and being humble does that.
CIPHER.518.question: Someone taped a piece of paper with this sequence onto the back of a trophy from last year's Science Olympiad. It's a quote by Andile Dlamini, written using a Porta Cipher with a keyword of UNLIKE. What does it say?
CIPHER.518.author: Andile Dlamini

CIPHER.496.cipherString: Remember that all models are wrong; the practical question is how wrong do they have to be to not be useful.
CIPHER.496.question: A strange code appears in the footer of a document you print from the lab computer. It's a quote by George E. P. Box, encoded in an Aristocrat. What does it say?
CIPHER.496.author: George E. P. Box

CIPHER.493.cipherString: There is hope and a kind of beauty in there somewhere, if you look for it.
CIPHER.493.question: Your Bunsen burner's flame suddenly spelled this Nihilist Substitution message by H. R. Giger with a keyword length of 5. The 20th through 29th cipher units (32 56 40 57 26 33 36 39 66 55) decode to be OFBEAUTYIN. What does it say?
CIPHER.493.author: H. R. Giger

CIPHER.504.cipherString: It is impossible to travel faster than light, and certainly not desirable, as one's hat keeps blowing off.
CIPHER.504.question: Astronomers notice this repeating sequence in deep-space data. It's actually a quote by Woody Allen, encoded with an Aristocrat. You are told that the cipher contains OFF. What does it say?
CIPHER.504.author: Woody Allen

CIPHER.499.question: Your chemistry lab's fume hood started beeping in Morse… but the real puzzle is this Cryptarithm quote. What do the values 458 10176 50329 decode to?

CIPHER.514.cipherString: There are only two lasting bequests we can hope to give our children. One of these is roots, the other, wings.
CIPHER.514.question: Archaeologists unearth a stone tablet inscribed with a message. It's a quote by Hodding Carter encoded as an Aristocrat using a K1 alphabet. What was the K1 Keyword used to encode it?
CIPHER.514.author: Hodding Carter

CIPHER.501.cipherString: DNA has been aptly described as the first three-dimensional Xerox machine.
CIPHER.501.question: You find a coded sequence scratched into the surface of a lab stool. It turns out to be a quote by Kenneth Boulding, written in a Checkerboard with a Polybius key of EARTHLINGS. What does it say?
CIPHER.501.author: Kenneth Boulding

CIPHER.508.cipherString: No matter how good an idea sounds, test it first.
CIPHER.508.question: A riddle is discovered taped under a chemistry lab bench. It's a quote by Henry Bloch, encrypted as a Baconian. You are told that the deciphered text starts with NOMA. What does it say?
CIPHER.508.author: Henry Bloch

CIPHER.517.cipherString: Regardless of how you perceive it, life is the greatest gift every human being has ever received. Be cheerful about it, and cherish it.
CIPHER.517.question: A note falls out of a borrowed library book. The message is a quote by Gift Gugu Mona, written as a Patristocrat Cipher using a K2 alphabet. You are told that the cipher ends with HIT. What does it say?
CIPHER.517.author: Gift Gugu Mona

CIPHER.500.cipherString: People never realize how much work impacts their self esteem and sense of purpose until they leave a job.
CIPHER.500.question: A coded message is discovered inside a fortune cookie. It turns out to be a quote by Rob Payne, encoded using an Aristocrat Cipher using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.500.author: Rob Payne

CIPHER.491.cipherString: No man is a hero to his valet.
CIPHER.491.question: Solve this quote by Georg Wilhelm Friedrich Hegel that has been encoded using the Hill Cipher with a keyword of FLEX. What does it say?
CIPHER.491.author: Georg Wilhelm Friedrich Hegel

CIPHER.509.cipherString: Can science ever be immune from experiments conceived out of prejudices and stereotypes, conscious or not?
CIPHER.509.question: A message was left on the whiteboard after science club. It's a quote encoded using an Aristocrat using a K2 alphabet. What does it say?

CIPHER.494.cipherString: The reader might reflect that an awful lot of supposing has to take place in order for the quantity theory of money to be true.
CIPHER.494.question: A weathered note is found inside an old science textbook. The writing appears to be a quote by Paul Ormerod encoded as a Patristocrat using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher starts with THE. What does it say?
CIPHER.494.author: Paul Ormerod

CIPHER.516.cipherString: Wonder and knowledge are both to be cherished.
CIPHER.516.question: You receive this cryptic message from the future through an online time capsule. It's a quote by Stephen Jay Gould, written in a Baconian. What does it say?
CIPHER.516.author: Stephen Jay Gould

CIPHER.510.cipherString: Life's like art. You have to work hard to keep it simple and still have meaning.
CIPHER.510.question: Scrawled in dry-erase marker on the whiteboard after hours: this Porta encoded quote by Charles de Lint. The 49th through 55th cipher characters (QYRUSGZ) decode to be ILLHAVE. What does it say?
CIPHER.510.author: Charles de Lint

CIPHER.498.cipherString: After one look at this planet any visitor from outer space would say I WANT TO SEE THE MANAGER.
CIPHER.498.question: While analyzing the background noise from a physics experiment, you detect this pattern. It's a quote, encoded using an Aristocrat using a K2 alphabet. What was the K2 Key Phrase used to encode it?

CIPHER.512.cipherString: He is best served who has no need to put other people's hands at the end of his arms.
CIPHER.512.question: You find this encoded line written in invisible ink on a lab safety poster. It's a quote by Rousseau, written in a Checkerboard. The 37th through 42nd cipher units (YS PS YK YS YI PS) decode to be PEOPLE. What does it say?
CIPHER.512.author: Rousseau

CIPHER.515.cipherString: How much the sea became very small, when the first ship sailed.
CIPHER.515.question: You came across the following written on a wall in a cave. You are told that it's a quote by Mohamed Ismail encoded as a Nihilist Substitution with a keyword of STAR and Polybius key of COMPLEXITY. What does it say?
CIPHER.515.author: Mohamed Ismail

CIPHER.503.cipherString: I feel like honesty gives people the freedom to talk about things they wouldn't otherwise.
CIPHER.503.question: Projected by a broken laser pointer during physics class, solve this Complete Columnar quote from Jane Fonda. The quote has OMTOT somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.503.author: Jane Fonda

CIPHER.513.cipherString: to start at a new place is always to feel incompetent and unwanted.
CIPHER.513.question: Your school-issued Chromebook's login screen hijacked itself with this Nihilist Substitution quote from Philip Larkin with a keyword of SIMPLY and Polybius key of LIFEGUARDS. What does it say?
CIPHER.513.author: Philip Larkin

CIPHER.507.cipherString: I think as a kid I never really understood the magnitude of a hurricane and what it could do.
CIPHER.507.question: A message in a bottle washed up in the school's decorative fountain—decode this Checkerboard cipher text by Marshall Faulk with a Polybius key of MARVELOUS. What does it say?
CIPHER.507.author: Marshall Faulk

CIPHER.497.cipherString: In the midst of life we are in debt.
CIPHER.497.question: Ethel Watts Mumford has been heard to say the following phrase that has been encoded using the Hill Cipher with a keyword of PROTOTYPE. What does it say?
CIPHER.497.author: Ethel Watts Mumford

CIPHER.492.cipherString: La satisfacción consiste en liberarse del dolor, que es el elemento positivo de la existencia.
CIPHER.492.question: Your lab partner's retainer case clicked open to reveal this Xenocrypt-encrypted quote by Schopenhauer in Spanish using a K2 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.492.author: Schopenhauer
CIPHER.492.translation: Satisfaction consists in freedom from pain, which is the positive element of existence.

CIPHER.511.question: Your lab partner discovered this pattern on the back of a graphing calculator. It's a quote, encoded as a Cryptarithm. What do the values 0 1082 934567 decode to?

CIPHER.502.cipherString: Infeliz es el hombre por quien su propia madre no ha hecho venerables a todas las demás madres.
CIPHER.502.question: A mysterious postcard arrives from an unknown sender. It contains a coded quote by RICHTER in Spanish, written in a Xenocrypt using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.502.author: RICHTER
CIPHER.502.translation: Unhappy is the man for whom his own mother has not made all other mothers venerable.
